title: tephroseris integrifolia subsp. caucasigena
intro: Tephroseris integrifolia subsp. caucasigena, also known as Caucasian Mountain Everlasting, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the Caucasus region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and possessing antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions and infections. The plant's medicinal properties can be utilized through various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create topical creams, ointments, and salves.
